meta.description: 4-day Hyderabad heritage tour with Charminar, Golconda, Nizami palaces, Salar Jung Museum and pearls. From RM898 per person.
meta.keywords: 4 Days Hyderabad Heritage Escape from Malaysia, India tour package, South India tour package Malaysia, Dhesu travel

hero.title: 4 Days in Hyderabad.
hero.intro: An extra day to do Hyderabad justice. The City of Pearls deserves more than a quick stop: Charminar, Golconda, the Nizami palaces, the pearl markets and the best biryani in India. Four days, properly savoured.

overview.heading: 4 Days Hyderabad Heritage Escape: The Full Picture
body: Hyderabad rewards a slower visit. The legacy of the Nizams, once among the richest rulers on earth, is everywhere: in the palaces, the pearl trade, the cuisine and the old city around Charminar. Four days lets you take it all in without rushing, including day trips and deeper exploration.

This 4 day Hyderabad heritage escape from Malaysia is private throughout, with your own vehicle and driver guide. Alongside the icons of Charminar and Golconda, the extra time allows for the Qutb Shahi tombs, the grand Chowmahalla and Falaknuma palaces, the Salar Jung Museum, and plenty of pearl shopping and biryani.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: Charminar, Golconda & the Tombs
items.body: The 16th-century Charminar, the mighty Golconda Fort with its famous acoustics, and the atmospheric Qutb Shahi tombs, the heart of historic Hyderabad.

items.title: Nizami Palaces & Museum
items.body: The opulent Chowmahalla Palace, the Falaknuma Palace high above the city, and the treasure-filled Salar Jung Museum reveal the wealth of the Nizams.

items.title: Pearls & the Famous Biryani
items.body: Shop for Hyderabad's renowned pearls, then feast on the city's legendary dum biryani. The extra day means time to do both properly.

days.title: Arrive Hyderabad
days.description: Met at the airport and transferred to your hotel. Evening at the Hussain Sagar lakefront.

days.title: Old City & Golconda
days.description: The old city, Charminar and Golconda Fort.

days.title: Palaces, Museum & Pearls
days.description: The Nizami palaces, museum and pearl markets.

days.title: Depart
days.description: Checkout and transfer to the airport for your onward flight.

whyBook.heading: Hyderabad in Depth, Handled Locally
whyBook.body: The Nizami sites are spread across the city, and the old town is dense. A private guide ties it together and gets you to the right biryani. Dhesu has run South India tours since 1988.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. We guide you through India e-Visa documentation with every booking.

points.title: Private Tours, Daily Departures
points.body: No minimum group to wait for. Your trip runs on your dates, your pace, your own vehicle and guide.

points.title: A Real Team to Call
points.body: Reachable throughout your trip. Flight delayed, your guide waits. Plans change, we adapt on the ground.

points.title: Group & Custom Pricing
points.body: Travelling as a family or group lowers the per-person cost. Ask us for a tailored quote.

sections.heading: Hyderabad Heritage Escape from Malaysia
sections.body:
This 4 day package gives Hyderabad the time it deserves. Beyond the icons of Charminar and Golconda, you explore the Nizami palaces, the Salar Jung Museum and the pearl markets at a relaxed pace.

A private vehicle and driver guide handle the city's spread-out heritage and the dense old town, with time for the famous biryani and shopping.

For a shorter visit, see our 3 day Hyderabad package; for more of the south, our Bangalore and Discover South India tours.

sections.heading: The Legacy of the Nizams
sections.body:
The Nizams of Hyderabad ruled one of the wealthiest princely states in India, and their legacy defines the city. The Chowmahalla and Falaknuma palaces, the Salar Jung Museum's vast collection, and the pearl and bangle trade all stem from this golden age.

The extra day in this package lets you explore that heritage properly, rather than rushing past the icons.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (HYD). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: What does the 4 day version add over the 3 day?
items.answer: The extra day allows for the Nizami palaces (Chowmahalla and Falaknuma), the Salar Jung Museum, and more time for pearl shopping and the old city, giving a fuller, more relaxed experience of Hyderabad's heritage.

items.question: Can I get halal food in Hyderabad?
items.answer: Easily. Hyderabad has a large Muslim community and a deep biryani culture rooted in it. Halal restaurants and biryani houses are plentiful, especially around the old city and Charminar.

items.question: What is the best time to visit Hyderabad?
items.answer: October to February offers the most comfortable weather for sightseeing. March to May is hot, and the monsoon (June to September) is moderate. Winter is ideal for exploring the old city and forts.
